true_relatives.note: The Sanskrit kaṭāha was a large iron vessel for cooking; कड़ाई is its direct descendant, still the deep iron pan used for frying and curries

sound_alikes.note: 'Karahi' or 'karai' is how English menus spell this very vessel — the dish 'chicken karahi' is named after the कड़ाई it's cooked in

root_breakdown.logic: The Sanskrit cauldron gradually became the iconic Indian deep-sided pan used for deep-frying and thick curries.

etymology_story: The Sanskrit kaṭāha was a large iron cauldron, the kind used for cooking at feasts or boiling large quantities.
Through Prakrit it became kaḍāha, retaining its sense of a deep, heavy vessel.
In Hindi it settled as कड़ाई — the round-bottomed, deep iron pan found in virtually every Indian kitchen.

pronunciation_guide.stress: stress on the long second syllable ṛā

tips.tip: English restaurant menus spell it 'karahi' or 'karai' — the pronunciation is almost identical

memory_hook.primary: A deep black iron pan with two handles, sitting over a flame with golden oil shimmering inside it
memory_hook.connection: The deep shape and the two side-handles are the defining visual features of a कड़ाई, distinguishing it from the flat तवा

examples.hindi: कड़ाई में तेल गरम करो।
examples.romanisation: kaṛāī mẽ tel garam karo.
examples.english: Heat oil in the wok.
examples.literal: wok in oil hot do

examples.hindi: माँ ने कड़ाई में समोसे तले।
examples.romanisation: mā̃ ne kaṛāī mẽ samose tale.
examples.english: Mum fried samosas in the deep pan.
examples.literal: mother by wok in samosas fried

examples.hindi: यह कड़ाई बहुत भारी है।
examples.romanisation: yah kaṛāī bahut bhārī hai.
examples.english: This wok is very heavy.
examples.literal: this wok very heavy is

examples.hindi: कढ़ाई चिकन एक मशहूर पकवान है।
examples.romanisation: kaṛhāī cikan ek mashhūr pakvān hai.
examples.english: Karahi chicken is a famous dish.
examples.literal: karahi chicken one famous dish is

true_relatives.note: Direct borrowing from English 'cooker', specifically meaning a pressure cooker in Indian usage

useful_coincidences.note: Both kuhnya and kukar share the same distant Proto-Indo-European cooking root — the ku/ku- sound links kitchen and cooking vessel across languages

sound_alikes.note: कुकर is simply 'cooker' in English — identical meaning and almost identical sound

root_breakdown.logic: The Latin root for cooking passed through English into Hindi, where 'cooker' became specifically associated with the pressure cooker — the most iconic cooking appliance in Indian kitchens.

etymology_story: The word traces back to Latin coquere, meaning to cook, which gave English 'cook' and then 'cooker'.
British colonial influence brought English kitchen vocabulary into Indian everyday speech.
In Hindi, कुकर became the standard word for the whistling pressure cooker — an appliance so central to Indian cooking that it needs no qualifier.

tips.tip: कुकर sounds just like the English 'cooker' — the only difference is Hindi uses a shorter 'u' vowel

memory_hook.primary: A silver pressure cooker with steam shooting from its weighted valve, sitting on a gas flame
memory_hook.connection: The image is the object itself — the quintessential Indian pressure cooker — and its name कुकर is its English label worn daily

examples.hindi: कुकर में दाल बना रही हूँ।
examples.romanisation: kukar mẽ dāl banā rahī hū̃.
examples.english: I am making lentils in the pressure cooker.
examples.literal: cooker in lentils making am

examples.hindi: कुकर की सीटी बज गई।
examples.romanisation: kukar kī sīṭī baj gaī.
examples.english: The pressure cooker whistled.
examples.literal: cooker of whistle rang went

examples.hindi: कुकर चूल्हे पर रखा है।
examples.romanisation: kukar cūlhe par rakhā hai.
examples.english: The pressure cooker is on the stove.
examples.literal: cooker stove on placed is

examples.hindi: इस कुकर में चावल जल्दी बन जाते हैं।
examples.romanisation: is kukar mẽ cāval jaldī ban jāte haĩ.
examples.english: Rice gets cooked quickly in this pressure cooker.
examples.literal: this cooker in rice quickly become go

true_relatives.note: Direct borrowing of English 'fridge', the informal short form of 'refrigerator'

useful_coincidences.note: The Russian word is built from 'cold' (khólod), making its logic transparent; Hindi took the English abbreviation instead, showing two different paths to the same appliance

sound_alikes.note: फ्रिज is simply the English 'fridge' — identical in sound and meaning

root_breakdown.logic: The Latin root for cold gave English 'refrigerator', which was shortened to 'fridge', then borrowed wholesale into Hindi.

etymology_story: Latin frigus meant cold or frost, and the word refrigerare meant to cool something down.
English formed 'refrigerator' from this root for the electric cooling appliance invented in the early twentieth century.
Hindi borrowed the colloquial English shortening 'fridge' directly, and फ्रिज is now universally understood across India.

tips.tip: फ्रिज sounds exactly like the English 'fridge' — the same consonant cluster and the same short 'i'

memory_hook.primary: A white refrigerator standing in a kitchen with the door open, cold air visibly misting out around shelves of vegetables and a jug of water
memory_hook.connection: The image is the appliance itself — the visual of cold air pouring out locks in both the meaning and the sound फ्रिज

examples.hindi: दूध फ्रिज में रखो।
examples.romanisation: dūdh frij mẽ rakho.
examples.english: Put the milk in the fridge.
examples.literal: milk fridge in place

examples.hindi: फ्रिज खाली है, सब्ज़ी लानी होगी।
examples.romanisation: frij khālī hai, sabzī lānī hogī.
examples.english: The fridge is empty; I'll have to get vegetables.
examples.literal: fridge empty is, vegetables bring will-have

examples.hindi: क्या फ्रिज में ठंडा पानी है?
examples.romanisation: kyā frij mẽ ṭhaṃḍā pānī hai?
examples.english: Is there cold water in the fridge?
examples.literal: what fridge in cold water is

examples.hindi: रात को खाना फ्रिज में रख दिया।
examples.romanisation: rāt ko khānā frij mẽ rakh diyā.
examples.english: I put the food in the fridge at night.
examples.literal: night to food fridge in placed gave

true_relatives.note: Hindi चूल्हा comes from the Sanskrit cullī — both describe the hearth or cooking fire, showing the unbroken connection between ancient and modern kitchens

sound_alikes.note: Ironically, चूल्हा starts with a 'cool' sound ('cū') yet means the hot stove — the irony is the hook: the 'cool' sound belongs to the hot place

root_breakdown.logic: The Sanskrit hearth — originally a clay fireplace in a home — became चूल्हा in Hindi, a word now used for anything from a traditional wood fire to a modern gas burner.

etymology_story: The Sanskrit cullī was the traditional earthen cooking hearth, the centre of every ancient Indian home's kitchen.
As the word evolved through Prakrit into Hindi, it became चूल्हा, keeping its meaning of the cooking fire.
Today चूल्हा covers both the old clay woodfire stove and the modern gas burner — the heart of the kitchen, ancient or modern.

pronunciation_guide.stress: stress on the first long syllable

tips.tip: the first syllable 'cūl' sounds like the English 'cool' — a long 'oo' sound, then add 'hā'

memory_hook.primary: A traditional clay stove with orange flames licking out from under a black pot, set into a mud kitchen floor
memory_hook.connection: The ancient clay hearth is the original meaning of चूल्हा — this image captures exactly what the word has meant for thousands of years

examples.hindi: चूल्हे पर तवा रखो।
examples.romanisation: cūlhe par tavā rakho.
examples.english: Put the griddle on the stove.
examples.literal: stove on griddle place

examples.hindi: गाँव में अभी भी लकड़ी का चूल्हा जलता है।
examples.romanisation: gā̃v mẽ abhī bhī lakṛī kā cūlhā jaltā hai.
examples.english: In the village they still use a wood-burning stove.
examples.literal: village in still also wood of stove burns

examples.hindi: कुकर चूल्हे पर चढ़ाओ।
examples.romanisation: kukar cūlhe par caṛhāo.
examples.english: Put the pressure cooker on the stove.
examples.literal: cooker stove on place-up

examples.hindi: चूल्हा बंद करना मत भूलना।
examples.romanisation: cūlhā band karnā mat bhūlnā.
examples.english: Don't forget to turn off the stove.
examples.literal: stove off doing not forget

true_relatives.note: Direct borrowing of English 'oven' into Hindi — same meaning, very similar sound

useful_coincidences.note: Russian uses a completely different word for oven, making ओवन the recognisably English-origin term in Indian kitchens

sound_alikes.note: ओवन is the English word 'oven' — same sound, same meaning, making it immediately recognisable

root_breakdown.logic: The ancient Proto-Germanic heating vessel became the English oven, and was then borrowed into Hindi as modern appliances entered Indian homes.

etymology_story: The word oven is one of the oldest Germanic household words, going back to Proto-Germanic *uhwaz meaning a kiln or enclosed hearth.
Old English had ofen for the baking chamber, which became 'oven' in modern English.
As electric and gas ovens became common in Indian urban homes, Hindi adopted the English word ओवन directly.

tips.tip: ओवन sounds like the English 'oven' — the 'o' is slightly longer in Hindi but otherwise identical

memory_hook.primary: A stainless steel oven door with a glass window, glowing orange inside, with a tray of bread visible through the glass
memory_hook.connection: The glowing interior seen through the oven window is the defining visual of ओवन — the enclosed hot space that bakes

examples.hindi: ओवन में केक रखो।
examples.romanisation: ovan mẽ kek rakho.
examples.english: Put the cake in the oven.
examples.literal: oven in cake place

examples.hindi: ओवन दो सौ डिग्री पर गरम करो।
examples.romanisation: ovan do sau ḍigrī par garam karo.
examples.english: Heat the oven to two hundred degrees.
examples.literal: oven two hundred degrees on hot do

examples.hindi: हमारे घर में ओवन नहीं है, चूल्हा है।
examples.romanisation: hamāre ghar mẽ ovan nahī̃ hai, cūlhā hai.
examples.english: We don't have an oven at home, we have a stove.
examples.literal: our home in oven not is, stove is

examples.hindi: ओवन में रोटी सेंकना नहीं, तवे पर सेंकना।
examples.romanisation: ovan mẽ roṭī sẽknā nahī̃, tave par sẽknā.
examples.english: Don't bake roti in the oven — bake it on the griddle.
examples.literal: oven in roti baking not, griddle on baking
